system: closed-closed acoustic tube truncated to three displacement modes with a pressure microphone observer

state_variables.name: xi1
state_variables.meaning: amplitude of first longitudinal displacement sine mode

state_variables.name: xi2
state_variables.meaning: amplitude of second longitudinal displacement sine mode

state_variables.name: xi3
state_variables.meaning: amplitude of third longitudinal displacement sine mode

ideal: pressure p at x0 = L_tube / 3 as a linear functional of xi1, xi2, xi3
practical: small microphone calibrated as a point pressure sensor at x0 = L_tube / 3 within the low-amplitude acoustic regime

regime_assumptions: uniform tube area and homogeneous medium
rigid closed ends impose zero longitudinal displacement at x = 0 and x = L_tube
orthonormal sine displacement basis phi_n(x) = sqrt(2 / L_tube) sin(n pi x / L_tube)
pressure microphone is approximated by p(x0) = -rho_air c_sound^2 d xi / dx evaluated at x0
radiation losses, viscosity, thermal losses, drive response, impedance matching, reflection imaging, and sensor noise are outside this card
no hidden-load call is licensed because no ceiling/reference is declared
equation_hook: U(xi) = 1/2 rho_air c_sound^2 A_tube integral_0^L (d xi / dx)^2 dx; p(x0) = -rho_air c_sound^2 sum_n xi_n d phi_n/dx at x0

units_and_dimensions.xi_n: m sqrt(m) under the declared orthonormal spatial basis convention
units_and_dimensions.C_n: Pa per (m sqrt(m)); pressure observer row for xi_n
units_and_dimensions.Phi_p: quotient stiffness for the declared pressure channel, not an acoustic impedance

coordinate_note: This card uses longitudinal displacement modal amplitudes as the state coordinates and a pressure microphone as the observer row. The pressure quotient precision is meaningful only for this declared pressure channel; it is not acoustic impedance, intensity, reflection coefficient, or full field reconstruction.

module_object.kind: finite acoustic modal stiffness Hessian observed through a pressure row
module_object.description: three-mode acoustic stiffness Hessian K with mass metric M metadata and a one-row pressure observer C

derivation_status: exact for the declared small-signal one-dimensional closed-tube model after basis choice and three-mode truncation

observer_map.description: observe pressure p at x0 = L_tube / 3 through the first three displacement modes

ceiling_or_reference.description: no visible-block ceiling or reference pressure protocol is declared; hidden-load is therefore refused by omission

positive_scope: This card shows that an acoustic resonator can expose a finite modal positive object through a realistic pressure sensor after boundary, basis, truncation, and sensor convention are declared.
boundary: It does not attach raw sound intensity, ultrasound reflection, acoustic impedance, damping, drive response, sensor noise, or full pressure-field reconstruction.
failure_mode: The attachment fails if the tube is not well approximated as one-dimensional and small-signal, if the rigid-end boundary is invalid, if the finite truncation is not declared, or if the microphone channel is treated as full modal access.
next_measurement: Measure the first three tube resonant frequencies and a calibrated point-pressure response at x0 = L_tube / 3 before adding damping, drive response, or noise.
